strengths: 19+ years of hands-on software engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backend systems (e.g., low-latency microservices handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby)
Entrepreneurial track record as founder of GISCollective, an open-source geo-data platform running on Kubernetes and used by Green Map
Demonstrated knowledge sharing and community leadership through conference talks (FOSS4G 2019), workshops, and multi-day courses
concerns: No evidence of director/principal-level scope: career history is Senior/Staff IC roles focused on feature delivery, not organization-wide technical strategy
AI experience is thin and recent — limited to MCP code generators and agent tooling since April 2025, with no shipped AI products or recognized AI expertise
No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field, and no cloud platform strategy or strategic customer advisory experience cited
response.justification: The candidate meets the minimum bar: a BS in Computer Science and roughly 19 years of software engineering experience spanning embedded (National Instruments), high-scale backend (Dunnhumby), and full-stack product work. Entrepreneurial experience exists via founding GISCollective. However, the role's core competencies — recognized AI expertise with demonstrated AI-built products, cloud/AI platform strategy, and director-level influence advising strategic customers — are largely absent. The only AI evidence is recent MCP/agent-tooling contributions at re:cinq (since April 2025), and the resume shows no advanced AI degree, no AI products at scale, and no strategic advisory or cross-org leadership roles. Strong senior engineer, but a poor fit for a Principal Engineer role centered on AI strategy.
